sources.label: AdvanSix DEF 14A proxy - virtual annual meeting June 22, 2026; FY25 results recap (StockTitan)
AdvanSix 8-K May 8, 2026 - Q1 results and $0.16 dividend (paid June 2)

verdicts.rankRationale: No material news since June 6 and a flat tape (-0.51% today); the latent-node case (caprolactam/ammonium sulfate chemistry adaptable to industrial-materials inputs) is unchanged from yesterday's hold, so no basis to re-rank on this strict-evidence page.
verdicts.bull: May 7 ammonia-platform process design and licensing agreement shows the chemistry franchise can be monetized beyond nylon, and at a $0.59B cap the name remains genuinely under-owned with optionality the market is not pricing.
verdicts.neutral: A commodity-ish specialty chemicals producer whose AI linkage is latent and speculative; +23% YTD largely reflects cyclical recovery and the dividend/value bid, not AI-chain evidence, so it drifts at rank 32 until real proof points emerge.
verdicts.bear: Q1 adjusted EPS of $0.50 vs $0.93 a year ago shows earnings pressure; if the cycle softens further the YTD gain unwinds and the AI-adjacency thesis never had hard evidence behind it.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Concrete industrial-materials or ammonia-licensing commercial wins (next checkpoint: Q2 earnings in early August; annual meeting June 22 is routine).
verdicts.primaryRisk: Cyclical nylon/caprolactam pricing weakness erodes earnings while the latent AI-supply-chain linkage remains unproven.
